provenance.source_corpus: Assessing the Effectiveness of a Constructed Arctic Stream Using Multiple Biological Attributes

source_spans.text: We suggest, however, that a scarcity of coarse organic matter is a major contributor to the low densities of macroinvertebrates inhabiting the constructed stream following its development
We suggest, however, that a scarcity of coarse organic matter is a major contributor to the low densities of macroinvertebrates inhabiting the constructed stream following its development

Low macroinvertebrate abundances and low coverage by macrophytes and bryophytes in the constructed stream may also have resulted because much of it was still in a phase of hydraulic flux, with marked erosion and deposition of river bed materials.

Low macroinvertebrate abundances and low coverage by macrophytes and bryophytes in the constructed stream may also have resulted because much of it was still in a phase of hydraulic flux, with marked erosion and deposition of river bed materials.

As well, the spring freshet is accompanied by active bed movement, sediment mobilization, bed scour, and high water turbidity (Jones and others 2003b), which are known to hamper macroinvertebrate establishment and growth (Waters 1995; Shaw and Richardson 2001).

As well, the spring freshet is accompanied by active bed movement, sediment mobilization, bed scour, and high water turbidity (Jones and others 2003b), which are known to hamper macroinvertebrate establishment and growth (Waters 1995; Shaw and Richardson 2001).

nodes.name: virtual lack of local riparian vegetation

source_spans.text: The virtual lack of local riparian vegetation to serve as a source pool for large woody debris and CPOM, combined with low retention of large woody materials, also precludes the establishment of snag-dwelling invertebrate communities (Benke and others 2001) that reside in natural streams.

The virtual lack of local riparian vegetation to serve as a source pool for large woody debris and CPOM, combined with low retention of large woody materials, also precludes the establishment of snag-dwelling invertebrate communities (Benke and others 2001) that reside in natural streams.
In turn, lower macroinvertebrate densities resulted in reduced forage base and growth of young-of-the-year Arctic grayling in the constructed stream (Jones and others 2003a, b).

nodes.name: reduced forage base and growth of young-of-the-year Arctic grayling

source_spans.text: In turn, lower macroinvertebrate densities resulted in reduced forage base and growth of young-of-the-year Arctic grayling in the constructed stream (Jones and others 2003a, b).

nodes.name: initial efforts to improve fish habitat

source_spans.text: Because initial efforts to improve fish habitat in the constructed stream focused on physical structures (e.g., weirs, vanes, rock, groins), ecological factors limiting fish growth were not considered and likely constrained success.

Because initial efforts to improve fish habitat in the constructed stream focused on physical structures (e.g., weirs, vanes, rock, groins), ecological factors limiting fish growth were not considered and likely constrained success.

nodes.name: The addition of woody debris and CPOM

source_spans.text: The addition of woody debris and CPOM, combined with planting of shrubs and herbs along the stream, should provide a source of allochthonous matter for the biotic community while large cobble and boulders should improve the physical stability of stream system, protecting its organic components.

The addition of woody debris and CPOM, combined with planting of shrubs and herbs along the stream, should provide a source of allochthonous matter for the biotic community while large cobble and boulders should improve the physical stability of stream system, protecting its organic components.
